rationale: Phase 1b B(5) implements conditional branches as slice-walker terminators. The walker (with the corrected emit contract) emits TranslateResult.pre_delay_code BEFORE the delay slot and the terminator c_code AFTER it. The strict translator's branch handlers populate pre_delay_code with `uint32_t psx_brA_<addr> = cpu->gpr[rs];` (and psx_brB_ for BEQ/BNE), and the c_code reads ONLY those snapshot locals. This makes the architectural rs/rt-before-delay rule correct for every branch regardless of what the delay slot writes. The `delay_slot_writes_branch_operand` count is an intrinsic property of the BIOS bytes (it cannot be reduced by translator work); `incorrect_in_current_translator` is the count that matters for correctness and must be 0.
